text:
the plan

time: 15:00

during the winter laura and i began talking about a family road trip. it had been six years since we made it all the way to santa monica -- sid and annie were 8 and 5 then -- and timing was once again favorable. we were thinking not nearly as ambitious this time, just 2 weeks instead of 5, and certainly less miles, in fact, there was already a destination in mind. not a focal point necessarily, but something to incorporate. off the beaten path, thus good for a road trip. far enough away, but not all the way across the country. a place i hadn't been to in half my life, yet promised to return to. it was "the rings."

the rings are in south dakota, at a place called evan's plunge. it's a series of olympic-style rings suspended over an olympic-style pool, built up around a hot spring, or at least a lukewarm spring, with water in the high 80s filtering through the pebble floor. on my first ever cross country trip i came across this place, and after watching the other kids i realized the goal was to swing over the pool -- ring to ring, platform to platform -- without falling in, american ninja warrior style. i was frustrated to discover i couldn't do it and vowed immediately to return. that was 24 years ago.

around march laura forwarded me an article that a psychologist had written about the concept of the "14 trip." the idea is that, when a kid reaches 14, the off parent, meaning the one that sees the kid less for whatever reason -- job, schedule, etc -- should take a 1 on 1 trip with that kid. 14 is apparently a good age to show them a world beyond the middle school hallways exists, and it just so happened sid was turning 14 this summer. not much time passed before we concluded the optimal configuration was for only sid and i to go, and for laura and annie to stay home. one nice thing about this was it sustained more ambition -- we could go a little further and for a little longer, and started to plan accordingly.

the article said it was important to favor authentic experiences, avoiding disney worlds, and to let the kid help plan. "i want to see mountains" was sid's first response to this, causing an instant itinerary rearrangement to incorporate glacier national park. some friends had praised its natural beauty the previous year, putting it on our radar. it would make the perfect turnaround point for this trip. a litany of dinosaur adventures were also planned. "every age is dinosaur age" sid recently confessed when i expressed uncertainty about having taken them to a dinosaur puppet show downtown.

the dates formed naturally. the start would be the final weekend in june -- launch was designed around a jon batiste concern in western mass, even though it never materialized for us -- and the end would be in the middle of july -- we wanted to be home for sid's birthday, or on sid's birthday, as it were. initially the thought was to do the rings on the way out, and watch fireworks over mount rushmore on the fourth of july, but a cursory check on the internet suggested there were no such fireworks, and so we reversed the current from clockwise to counterclockwise, planning to find small town fireworks in montana, east of the mountains.

 in accordance with the philosophy espoused in the article, we avoided the disney worlds in favor of authentic experiences aligned with sid's interests.

i worked the day we left, a frantic morning elaborating on design ideas for the project i was leaving behind for six weeks, plus two days prior i had delivered the final readout to the customer on the pilot project we were running, which all together had me feeling like i was leaving work in a good state, and to be honest it hadn't really felt that way for most of the preceding months. it was impossible to process the relief and the freedom.

reese's minis

laura and the kids got me a bag of reese's minis for father's day that mostly sat around in the intervening week plus, but no way we were going to leave them behind as we set off on this adventure. the perfect console companion.

date: 2024-07-07
woke up at our campsite in dinosaur provincial park. drove impossibly straight and flat roads up to closer to 52 than 51 north latitude before rotating 90 degrees at hanna on the way to drumheller. from there we went to calgary, one of those canadian island metropolises with nothing around it but a mountain backdrop in this case. stopped downtown for lunch at a pretty fancy restaurant with open walls and then left for montana. drove the deerhoof trail -- intermittently a highway, at times the main thoroughfare of small towns -- back to the border, eagerly anticipating the ride across glacier national park along going to the sun highway. it was better than anticipated. steep walls, incredible vistas, waterfalls dressing the inner wall.

text:
plymouth, ma

drove by the waterfront at the tail end of the journey to give mama and annie time to prepare the lobsters and cake
montana tshirt w/fish and cowboy hat

massachusetts

a drive to the border and a drive back, with a bit of embellishment on the ride back to delay our arrival a few minutes until the lobsters were ready

new york

staying at greg and laura's, south troy diner in the morning, most of the first day of driving, then on the way back coming in from niagra over the rainbow bridge, staying at rob+sue's, pizza in the garage, soccer on the tv, sleeping in the basement, netflix, some piano in the front room, famous doughnuts, albany, south troy closed, mcdonalds

pennsylvania

just a short driving stint around erie

ohio

cleveland -- rock and roll, 21st floor, epic tacos, walking through downtown, the italian restaurant by the river bend / bridges, mitchell's ice cream

michigan

detroit, driving around downtown, looking for lunch, andrews on the corner, driving north, the mackinack bridge, getting out along lake michigan and the moth, the retreat, staying there, dirt roads across the upper peninsula in the morning, the hike to chapel falls and chapel rock in pictured rocks state park, then back, driving the shore west, marquette and little caesars, continuing west, dq

wisconsin

sort of gray, along lake superior but not much of distinction, a few small towns but not many

minnesota

hotel in duluth, driving, pines becoming thinner pines and ultimately fields and farms, clouds and rain becoming puffy clouds in blue sky and sunny

north dakota

grand forks drive by, route 2, devil's lake, ice cream, rugby, geographic center of north america, dollar store, minot, williston, mcdonalds

montana

finding sidney, the hiline, havre, beaver creek park -- our first camping -- a rainy ride to fort benton, the park, the b social bar and bowling, great falls, 4th of july street celebration breaking up, american reunion on the hotel tv, the ride to the border crossing, going to the sun road (twice), camping in west glacier, the hike to avalanche lake, driving by flathead lake, tamarack in missoula, ice cream in last chance gulch in helena, forest fire, bunkouse brewing, bozeman, billings, broadus.

saskatchewan

the border, the rain, the unpaved road until consul, the bakery, the rain clearing heading north to maple creek, just missed the farmer's market, catching up with the trans canadian and heading west

alberta

wild rose country, dinosaur provincial park and everything we did there, the big flat ride to hanna then west to drumheller, the dino museum then on to calgary, mountains, cactus cafe, heading south for the border and glacier, the deerhoof trail

wyoming

mostly devil's tower, short bits of driving before and afterwards, winding roads, hilly but not mountainous, for the most part.

south dakota

deadwood, sturgis, iron horse saloon, hot springs, evan's plunge, oil change, racing east, stealing glances of the badlands, darkness, 1/2 hour north to pierre, pulling in in the darkness, the lights on the horizon like stars. out in the morning, drove the remaining half of the state.

iowa

started with sioux city, the italian restaurant in the department store then the elevated connected walkways, then a drive on 20 across most of the state before bending into cedar rapids traffic and staying in the quad cities.

illinois

drove its width in the morning to get to chicago for a walk around town, grant park, large animated faces, water feature, food trucks, tree garden, then white sox game.

indiana

not much, really. a segment of highway sid couldn't possibly remember, wholly unremarkable. for my part i didn't know this particular mostly elevated and oddly empty tollway even existed.

ontario

drove the 401 and the qew, detour in london trying to find memories from my first job, eventually reached niagra and spent some time there before crossing back.

saratoga springs, ny

didn't drive through on the way to greg and laura's like i usually do, followed the map instead to maintain the 8p arrival time it was predicting. drove through on the way out in the morning, though.

troy, ny

south troy diner ! drove through campus on the way there. tried to go to south troy diner again on the last day, on the way home, but they were closed on mondays, so i relented and let sid have mcdonalds. i mean, it was her birthday.

clifton park, ny

greg in the wild ! met him at his office to retrieve the toiletries we'd left. he wears shorts and a tucked in golf style shirt to work apparently.

schenectady, ny

drive through, some nostalgia, didn't frequent that town during college and shortly thereafter but just enough to make it special, oddly.

cleveland, oh

21st floor, special parking spot, walking to the italian place down by the river bend and bridges, rock and roll hall of fame, epic tacos, shopping for supplies in regular old supermarkets, ran while sid rested, mitchell's ice cream, 21st floor !

detroit, mi

first time being there, airport aside. left the interstate as we were almost among the skyscrapers, caught a bit of the outskirts, little caesar's headquarters, public art, the baseball stadium, tried to get food but the menu was too fancy and nothing good for sid so we simply bowed out, thankfully because then we found andrews on the corner which was great. on the way home we once again drove through detroit, took one of the bridges to canada.

marquette, mi

the centerpiece of vacationland, not maine but the upper peninsula. thriving town center. got little caesars.

ashland, wi

up and down a few blocks, felt like a really isolated municipality with big farming implications however on lake superior.

superior, wi

the descent and a sense of suburban commerce and residential on the way into duluth.

duluth, mn

cloudy skies, daylight waning. a strange configuration of enormous port, massive highway construction, ultimately a few downtown blocks pressed between a rock backdrop and lake superior, a pro-palestinian demonstration on the wide stairs of a regal building, and then backtracking to the hotel that sid liked from the app. did laundry in the morning.

grand forks, nd

drive through. sid was just waking. confirmation i was looking at the same park i had visited in 2008.

devils lake, nd

there was the highway flank on the approach but then there was also a cluster of blocks around historic "fourth and fourth" where the gas station attendant told us we might be able to find ice cream.

rugby, nd

geographic center of north america. exactly the type of quirky thing you want in a road trip.

minot, nd

the mystique of minot preceded it, even though it wasn't so mysterious. some drive by photos and a gas stop.

williston, nd

oil town. gave sid a lot of chances to agree to stay for the night, but we pressed on. let her get mcdonalds, too.

culbertson, mt

had staked a lot on a park the maps noted had free camping, but didn't feel comfortable enough when we got there. the motels were uninviting, one even said "by reservation only" taped to the door. fought with it for 15 or 20 minutes before heading to sidney.

sidney, mt

sydney's namesake ! our emergency stop if you want to call it that. basically our recourse when the camping situation at culbertson didn't pan out. fortunately the town was substantial enough to have a nice motel. got a real warm vibe from the young woman taking my call and recognized the voice when we finally got to check in. supermarket in the morning, beautiful sunny day.

glasgow, mt

largest town on the hiline, at least that we passed through that morning. pulled down a couple side streets for some road photos.

malta, mt

the big man in the small dino museum.

chinook, mt

last hiline town before havre.

havre, mt

i felt compelled to will this town into the list of cutest small towns in montana next time they take the survey. we left the hiline for beaver creek part the afternoon we arrived, then came back for smores supplies in the evening. the next morning on the way to great falls we stopped a couple more times, including to shop for clothes and souvenirs at sal's, and the morning after that we stopped in again to tank up before breaking north toward the nearest border crossing.

box elder, mt

drive through havre -> fort benton.

big sandy, mt

small pass through on the way to fort benton and great falls from havre on the fourth of july. stopped for supplies. home of jon tester ?

fort benton, mt

the 13th and final cutest small town in montana and we made the most of it. the missouri riverfront park, the b social restaurant bar slash bowling alley. the lights were off in the alley but that was only to conserve electricity, they turned them on for us an we played a string. had great conversation with the locals, some of who were planning their first visit to banff this coming weekend. a nice guy named mike from havre talked me through the photos pinned up on the wall. sid signed a dollar that should still be there next time we go back, unless they were lying.

great falls, mt

an unanticipated hub of the trip. when we couldn't find an available site in beaver creek park nor an available hotel room in havre we headed there, also in part because we figured the bigger town would have more fireworks. later in the trip we came back in from the west to visit urgent care and ate at tracy's.

consul, sk

that bakery ! also where the dirt roads became paved, thank goodness, and the torrential storm basically ended for us.

maple creek, sk

the largest municipality we encountered in saskatchewan, but that's not to say much. the rains had just come through. there was a farmer's market closing up at around 2 in the afternoon as we were arriving.

medicine hat, ab

the library, the esplinade, the museum about the history of medicine hat within, the white floaty flora falling everywhere, an altogether beautiful day and a very nice city from the looks of it.

hanna, ab

proud home of nickelback, not much else it seems. i felt strangely compelled to drive a bit out of the way from dinosaur provincial park to drumheller by way of this town to experience the flat plains that the straight roads on the map suggested, and indeed it was big and flat in that part of the world.

calgary, ab

one of those canadian "island cities" in that it's basically self contained and has skyscrapers and a river running through it and a sense of youthful adventurism and there was some kind of stampede days going on. lots of people wearing jeans and tucked in shirts and cowboy hats. ate at a fancy-ish restaurant in the center of town and lamented we hadn't found some of the earthier places we passed on the way out of town before eating.

nanton, ab

another southern alberta town like cardston but it had an old plane propped up as a sort of monument.

cardston, ab

like a dream the way the highway degraded to a town thoroughfare then reconstituted itself afterwards, headed for glacier parallel to the mountains off to the right, trying to beat sunset.

whitefish, mt

the number one cutest small town in montana by the magazine article we'd been abiding by so had to check it and cute indeed. perfect looking breakfast place was unfortunately closed, walked a bit, drove over to mudman burger on the advice of the woman at the chamber of commerce. drove by the lake and beach on the way out.

kalispell, mt

was going to be a drive by but then we saw wheat montana which the girl at urgent care said was a montana-y place to eat so had to stop and get a pastry and a cookie.

missoula, mt

photo evidence (and koozies) from the rhinocerous bar, where shawn alicia and i spent our last night on the road on our way to seattle in 2001, then lunch in the tamarack. soccer on the big tv. went inside the bbq place next door first but the menu wasn't as agreeable.

helena, mt

spiritual rendezvous ahead of the decent from the moutain in from the west, then ice cream in last chance gulch followed by a small amount of driving around, another state capital photo op.

bozeman, mt

the approach on the straight roads west of town, surprising how much buildup there was, and traffic, too, for that matter. the road to yellowstone ? then bunkhouse brewing. then a ride through downtown, really nice there.

billings, mt

mostly just a place to sleep on the way to devil's tower from glacier and points west. break from the interstate. had been considering finding the retail store selling the geo map of montana we'd seen in two different dino museums, but enthusiasm waned in favor of an early start.

broadus, mt

a turning point on the ride from billings to devil's tower, had an outsized impression of the town, perhaps because of the older woman running the store where we bought the same tshirt as a sourvenir that she was wearing, the yellow one with the flowers and "montana" on it.

deadwood, sd

another drive by of a place i'd been in a previous life, what a difference a popular television show makes.

sturgis, sd

the motorcycle city. the iron horse saloon for lunch. it was really hot there and i accidentally left my wallet in the car during lunch and had to run across town to grab it while sid held down the table. pretty empty in there. basically left her as collateral.

hot springs, sd

evans plunge. the town itself had a more interesting shape than i remembered too.

rapid city, sd

oil change, mostly, plus the nav to get there and departing straight east to put in a few hours.

pierre, sd

late evening arrival, hotel stay, drive through in the morning, state house, baseball field.

sioux city, ia

ate at that department store turned pasta restaurant run by two men of indian ethnicity. then the elevated walkway tour, including the career academy and that quirky museum of sorts.

cedar rapids, ia

a drive through at rush hour, surprised at how substantial the city and its rush hour were.

quad cities, ia

just a place to sleep, really. tried to go out to do laundry but was pretty uncomfortable leaving sid for so long.

chicago, il

the parking app, walking among the tall buildings, the iron girders of the elevated train, grant park, the tv images of the faces, the water feature, the food trucks, then later the white sox game on the south side in the midst of their historic losing season

ann arbor, mi

mostly just buffalo wild wings, lots of sports paraphernalia, the hotel lobby was a bit hectic on account of a party

london, on

drive through in the rain, looking for that one north south that i used to frequent for dinner 25 years prior while visiting our development office for work

niagra, on

resort town turned amusement park, ice cream and churros at sweet jesus, the fish tanks in the rainforest cafe, the indian man taking my money was ok with american but gave me a canadian ten in change that i never got to use or exchange, the view was better from this side but prob could have been better if we got closer

buffalo, ny

rob and sue's mostly, plus famous doughnuts, some neighborhood driving in between

albany, ny

got off the interstate to pull a driveby of the suny a campus i attended but couldn't confirm i found it, maybe it's just different now ? took lark st, rapid fire college memories, took note of the gentrification on the way to menands and the south troy diner north of town

text: give me the night -- george benson
data:
{
    "id": 33,
